parts.answer:  ( A Salt bridge containing a solution of) potassium nitrate / KNO (1)  ( B Electrode made of ) platinum / Pt (1)  ( C Solution containing) iron(II) and iron(III) ions / Fe 2+ and Fe 3+ (ions) (1) Ignore any conditions, including concentrations Allow potassium chloride / KCl / sodium nitrate / NaNO / sodium chloride / NaCl Allow ammonium salts Do not award iron Allow soluble compounds of iron(II) and iron(III) e.g. chlorides, nitrates or sulfates Ignore acid (3) Question number Additional guidance Mark

 half-equation for bismuthate ions (1)  half-equation for manganate(VII) ions (1)  overall equation (1) Examples of equations: BiO − + 6H + + 2e − → Bi 3+ + 3H O Allow half-equation written in reverse Mn 2+ + 4H O → MnO − + 8H + + 5e − Allow − 5e − on left Allow half-equation written in reverse Stand alone mark 2Mn 2+ + 5BiO − +14H + → 2MnO − + 5Bi 3+ + 7H O Overall equation must be written in direction shown Allow multiples Do not award uncancelled electrons / H + / H O Allow ⇌ in equations Ignore state symbols even if incorrect (3) Question number Additional guidance Mark

 substitution of values into formula (1)  calculation of E (1) Example of calculation: E = − 0.74 + 8.31 x 298 x ln 0.0100 96500 x 3 E = − 0.77939 / − 0.7794 / − 0.779 / − 0.78 (V) TE on incorrect numbers in correct formula e.g. if [Cr 3+ ] = 0.100, E = − 0.76 (V) No TE on incorrect formula Ignore SF except 1 SF Ignore units, even if incorrect Correct answer with no working scores (2) (2) (Total for Question 21 = 8 marks) Question number Additional guidance Mark An answer that makes reference to the following points:  the curly arrow should go (from the benzene ring/ π bond / delocalised electrons / inside the hexagon and) towards the nitrogen / NO + (1)  the open end of the ‘horseshoe’ should be pointing towards the tetrahedral carbon / carbon with 4 bonds (1)  the curly arrow should start from the (C-H) bond (1) Allow the changes in any order Allow the changes shown in diagrams / amended diagrams in the question Penalise any additional incorrect changes Allow first arrow must be reversed Ignore just ‘the curly arrow is incorrect’ Ignore just ‘the curly arrow should not start from the hydrogen atom’ / ‘ the curly arrow is incorrect’ Ignore use of ion / molecule for hydrogen atom (3) Question number Additional guidance Mark  tin and (concentrated) hydrochloric acid / (concentrated) HCl((aq)) Allow just ‘HCl’ for hydrochloric acid Allow iron and (concentrated) hydrochloric acid / (concentrated) HCl((aq)) Ignore addition of sodium hydroxide / NaOH / alkali added after the acid Ignore mention of heat / catalyst Do not award dilute acid / sulfuric acid / nitric acid (1) Question Number Additional guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  the lone pair (of electrons) on the nitrogen atom  overlaps with π cloud / delocalised electrons / delocalise d system or interacts with (benzene) ring / delocalised electrons / delocalised system  so the nitrogen atom is less able to accept a hydrogen ion / H + / proton (1) (1) (1) Allow pair

 other optical isomer Example of optical isomer: The groups must be joined in the correct bonds around the central carbon atom but ignore the connectivity of the groups Allow the mirror images of the symbols Allow subscripts the other side of the symbols e.g. H C HC (1) (Total for Question 22 = 13 marks) Question number Additional guidance Mark

 expression for volume of oxygen reacting with CH ( 1)  expression for volume of oxygen reacting with C H (1)  calculation of volume of methane (1)  calculation of percentage of methane in mixture (1) Example of calculation: Let x cm be the volume of methane CH + 2O → CO + 2H O x cm of CH reacts with 2x cm of O C H + 3½O → 2CO + 3H O (25 – x) cm C H reacts with 3½(25 - x)cm O 2x + 3½(25 - x) = 65 x = 15 cm 15 x 100 = 60% 25 TE on volume of methane Correct answer with no working scores (4) Ignore SF Allow alternative methods e.g. 1 ratio CH : O = 1 : 2 (1) / CH + 2O → CO + 2H O ratio C H : O = 1 : 3.5 / 2 : 7 (1) / C H + 3½O → 2CO + 3H O (n = fraction of CH4) 2n + 3.5(1 – n) = 65 / 2.6 (1) 25 n = 0.9 / 0.6 so 60% methane (1) 15 e.g. 2 mol (CH + C H ) = 25 = 0.0010412/1.0412 x 10 − (1 ) 24000 mol O = 65 = 0.0027083 / 2.7083 x 10 − (1) 24000 ratio mol (CH + C H ) : mol O = 1 : 2.6 (1) so 60% methane (1) (4) Question number Additional guidance Mark

Step 1  potassium dichromate((VI)) and dilute sulfuric acid / acidified (potassium) dichromate((VI)) (and heat) (1)  equation (1) Step 2  hydrogen cyanide and potassium cyanide / cyanide ions or potassium cyanide and (sulfuric) acid / hydrogen ions or potassium cyanide and pH 8-10 / alkali (1)  equation (1) Step 3  lithium tetrahydridoaluminate((III)) / lithium aluminium hydride and dry ether / ethoxyethane (followed by a dilute acid) or hydrogen and nickel / platinum / palladium or sodium and ethanol (1)  equation (1) Allow correct formulae for all reagents Allow any combination of structural and displayed formulae in equations or skeletal formulae Example of equation for Step 1: Ignore missing H O from equation Reagents for Step 2 conditional on a carbonyl compound Example of equation for Step 2: Reagents for Step 3 conditional on a nitrile Example of equation for Step 3: Allow other correct balanced equations / 4[H] on arrow (6) (Total for Question 23 = 10 marks) Question number Additional guidance Mark  correct formula of iron(III) hydroxide (1)  rest of equation correct, conditional on correct precipitate (1) Examples of equation: [Fe(H O) ] 3+ + 3NH → Fe(OH) + 3H O + 3NH + or [Fe(H O) ] 3+ + 3NH → Fe(OH) (H O) + 3NH + Allow Fe(H O) (OH) Ignore state symbols, even if incorrect Ignore square brackets around iron(III) hydroxide formulae (2) Question number Additional guidance Mark  ligand exchange / ligand substitution / ligand displacement Allow ligand replacement Do not award ligand change / change in co-ordination number / redox / deprotonation in addition to correct answer (1) Question number Additional guidance Mark  6 bonds between N in diamines and Fe (1)  rest of diagram correct (1) Example of diagram: Allow NH − Fe on left of structure Conditional on 6 N-Fe bonds Allow C H for CH , H N for NH etc Allow displayed / skeletal formulae for ligands Ignore bond lengths and bond angles Ignore missing brackets and charge / 3+ on Fe Ignore lone pairs o

 correct equation Example of equation: 2HoF + 3Ca → 2Ho + 3CaF Allow multiples Ignore state symbols, even if incorrect (1) Question number Additional guidance Mark  there is extra stability associated with a half-filled (f-)subshell / one electron in each f orbital Allow 4f is more stable than 4f Allow to reduce the repulsion between paired electrons/ electron-electron repulsion (in orbitals) Do not award a half-filled f orbital (1) Question number Additional guidance Mark  ([Xe])4f Allow 1s 2s 2p 3s 3p 3d 4s 4p 4d 5s 5p 4f Allow ([Xe])4f 6s (1) Question number Additional guidance Mark An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  thulium (ion)/Tm 3+ has more protons (in the nucleus than cerium ion / Ce 3+ ) (1) EITHER  outer electrons are in the same (sub)shell OR so there will be a greater attraction between the nucleus / protons and the (outer) electrons / outer shell (1) Allow Tm 3+ has a greater nuclear charge (than Ce 3+ ) Ignore references to increasing atomic number / charge density Allow f sub-shell Allow same / similar shielding (2) Question number Additional guidance Mark  the lanthanide ions are larger than the transition metal ions (so there is space for more ligands) or there are more orbitals available to accept the lone pairs (from the ligands) (1) Question number Additional guidance Mark

An explanation that makes reference to the following points:  there are no f electrons in La 3+ ions (1)  so no f-f transitions can take place (1) Allow La 3+ has the same electronic configuration as Xe Allow no occupied f orbitals Allow f subshell / f orbital(s) are empty Ignore reference to numbers of electrons in other orbitals even if incorrect Do not award the difference in energy is outside the visible region Do not award the f-subshell does not split Stand alone mark (2) Question number Additional guidance Mark  calculation of moles of each element (1)  calculation of empirical formula (1)  overall formula (1) Example of calculation: Ce N H O moles 23.97 = 0.171 19.18 = 1.37 2.05 = 2.05 54.80 = 3.425 divide by smallest 0.171 0.171 = 1 1.37 0.171 = 8 2.05 0.171 = 12 3.425 0.171 = 20 Empirical formula CeN H O TE on mol ratio from M1 Example of overall formula: Ce(NH ) (NO ) .2H O or Ce(NO ) .(NH NO ) 2. 2H O or Ce(NO ) .2(NH NO ).2H O TE on M2 Allow the ions in any order / charges shown by the ions / missing dot(s) (3) Question number Additional guidance Mark  identification of X (1) Justification  X is an alcohol as it gives a red colour with cerium(IV) ammonium nitrate (1)  X is a tertiary alcohol / not a primary or a secondary alcohol as it does not react with acidified potassium dichromate(VI) (1)  X has 4 different groups attached to one carbon atom / has a chiral centre / carbon (atom) (1) Examples of structure of X : or Allow any unambiguous structure, including C H /C H groups, displayed / skeletal formulae Ignore connectivity of OH except OH-C on left Allow X is an alcohol as it has general formula C n H 2n+1 OH Ignore ketone (4) Question number Additional guidance Mark

 calculation of amount of Ce 4+ used (1)  calculation of amount of 4-aminophenol in 25.0 cm (1)  calculation of amount of 4-aminophenol in 100 cm (1)  calculation of mass of paracetamol (1)  calculation of percentage of paracetamol and answer given to 2 or 3SF (1) Example of calculation: Amount of Ce 4+ used = 21.70 x 0.100 1000 = 0.00217 / = 2.17 x 10 − (mol) Amount of 4-aminophenol in 25 cm = 0.00217 2 = 0.001085 / = 1.085 x 10 − (mol) TE on amount of Ce 4+ used Amount of 4-aminophenol in 100 cm = = 0.001085 x 4 = 0.00434 / = 4.34 x 10 − (mol) TE on amount of 4-aminophenol in 25 cm Allow M3 and M2 in reverse order (Amount paracetamol in tablet = amount of 4-aminophenol in 100 cm ) Mass of paracetamol = 0.00434 x 151 = 0.65534 (g) TE on amount of 4-aminophenol in 100 cm Percentage of paracetamol = 0.65534 x 100 0.800 = 82 / 81.9% TE on mass of paracetamol provided 0.800 is the denominator and answer < 100% Correct answer given to 2 or 3SF with no working scores (5) (5) (Total for Question 25 = 20 marks) Total for Section C = 20 marks Total for Paper = 90 marks Pearson Education Limited.
